template_block.name: debate-engine-run-summary
template_block.title: DEBATE ENGINE — Run Summary
template_block.statement: TEMPLATE INSTRUCTIONS This file is the standalone summary produced after a debate engine run completes. It is separate from the full transcript (debate-transcript-[slug].xml) and serves two purposes:

checklist.entry: A readable summary for decision owners and stakeholders who need the outcome without the full record.
A quick-reference companion to the full transcript for programme and governance use.

template_block.statement: It does not replace the full transcript. The full transcript is the audit record. If this summary and the transcript conflict, the transcript governs.
Complete every section. Do not omit sections for brevity.

Date: [YYYY-MM-DD] Service / programme: [Service name and programme name] Question debated: [Restate the exact question from the transcript Phase 1] Full transcript reference: [Filename of the full transcript, e.g., debate-transcript-ai-benefits-interface.xml] Decision owner (SRO): [Name and role — or "TBC" if not yet confirmed]

template_block.name: configuration
template_block.title: Configuration
template_block.statement: Participants: [LIST abbreviations and role names, e.g., UR (User Researcher), BA (Business Analyst) ...] Round count: [N] rounds Complexity mean: [X.XX] | Standard deviation: [X.XX] | Distinct opening positions: [N] Stress-test triggered: [Yes — [N] Round 1 positions converged / No — convergence did not occur or Round 1 had fewer than 3 distinct positions] Early termination: [State trigger if applicable, or "No — full round count completed"]

template_block.name: how-the-debate-moved
template_block.title: How the Debate Moved
template_block.statement: This section is a narrative account of the debate's trajectory. It is not a transcript summary. It should read as an analytical account of what changed minds and why, not as a list of who said what. Structure: opening landscape → key exchanges → convergence or deadlock → stress-test findings (if any). Aim for 300–500 words.

template_block.name: opening-landscape
template_block.title: Opening Landscape
template_block.statement: [DESCRIBE the range of Round 1 positions. Name the distinct positions and which participants held them. Identify any positions that were surprising, underrepresented, or significantly at odds with the others.]

template_block.name: key-exchanges
template_block.title: Key Exchanges
template_block.statement: [IDENTIFY 2–4 exchanges that most significantly shifted the debate. For each:

checklist.entry: State what the challenge or argument was
State what moved as a result
Explain why this was consequential — what would the debate have missed if this exchange had not happened?]

template_block.name: convergence-or-deadlock
template_block.title: Convergence or Deadlock
template_block.statement: [DESCRIBE the state of the debate when it closed. If convergence: state what participants converged on, how many positions collapsed into it, and whether any participants held out. If deadlock: state the crux and who held each side.]

template_block.name: stress-test-findings-if-applicable
template_block.title: Stress-Test Findings (if applicable)
template_block.statement: [If a stress-test was run: SUMMARISE what the two adversarial contributions argued, what evidence they identified as falsifying the consensus, and which user population they named as most disadvantaged. Assess whether these arguments changed anything for the panel — and if not, explain why.]

template_block.name: the-recommendation
template_block.title: The Recommendation
template_block.statement: State the panel's recommendation and ranking clearly. This section must be usable by a decision owner who has not read the full transcript. Do not use jargon without definition. Do not assume familiarity with the debate participants' arguments.

template_block.name: viable-options
template_block.title: Viable Options
template_block.statement: [LIST all options assessed by the panel. State their viability status clearly.]

checklist.entry: Option [A] — [Name]: [Viable / Not viable — one sentence stating why]
Option [B] — [Name]: [Viable / Not viable — one sentence. If viable with conditions, list the conditions.]

template_block.name: ranked-recommendation
template_block.title: Ranked Recommendation
template_block.statement: 1. [RECOMMENDED OPTION] — [recommended for / recommended as, e.g., "recommended for alpha development and testing"]

[ONE PARAGRAPH explaining the recommendation. State what criteria it best addresses. State what the evidence base is. State the strongest argument against it and why the recommendation holds despite that argument. Do not exceed 200 words.]
2. [SECOND OPTION] — [e.g., "viable fallback and current default"]
[ONE PARAGRAPH on the second ranking. State when or under what conditions this option should be preferred.]
[Continue for all viable options]

template_block.name: key-conditions-on-the-recommendation
template_block.title: Key Conditions on the Recommendation
template_block.statement: List the specific conditions attached to the recommendation. These are non-optional. If a condition is not met, the recommendation changes. State what it changes to.

row.cell: [CONDITION — be specific, e.g., "DPIA completed before first participant sessions"]
[Pre-alpha gate / Alpha exit gate / Beta deployment gate]
[STATE what the team should do if this condition cannot be met — e.g., "Revert to Option A until condition is satisfied"]

template_block.statement: [Repeat for all conditions]

template_block.name: critical-failure-modes
template_block.title: Critical Failure Modes
template_block.statement: Taken from Panel Section 3. State the 2–3 most serious failure modes for the recommended option. This section must be honest — the panel is arguing against its own recommendation here. Do not soften or omit failure modes because they are uncomfortable.

row.cell: [One sentence: what goes wrong and how]
[One sentence: what observable signal indicates this is happening]

template_block.statement: [Repeat for critical failure modes — minimum 2]
The user population worst served if the recommendation fails: [NAME and describe the population. State the mechanism of harm. Connect to the stress-test finding if one was run.]

template_block.name: escalations-to-the-sro
template_block.title: Escalations to the SRO
template_block.statement: Items requiring active human decision. The panel recommends; the SRO decides. Each escalation must state what the SRO needs to decide, by when, and what the consequence of inaction is.

row.cell: [What the SRO must actively decide or confirm]
[Before alpha / Before beta / Before deployment / By [date]]
[What happens if this is not resolved — state the specific risk or the fallback position]

template_block.statement: [Add rows as needed]

template_block.name: flags-for-awareness
template_block.title: Flags for Awareness
template_block.statement: Items the SRO should be aware of but that do not require an active decision right now.

row.cell: [One sentence on what the team should monitor or address]

template_block.statement: [Add rows as needed]

template_block.name: absent-voices-material-gaps
template_block.title: Absent Voices — Material Gaps
template_block.statement: Roles not in the debate whose absence affects the recommendation's completeness.

row.cell: [One sentence: what this role would have contributed that was not covered]
[Specific action — e.g., "Include in pre-alpha sprint team" or "Legal Specialist to review before alpha"]

template_block.statement: [Add rows as needed. If no material gaps, state "No material absent-voice gaps identified."]

columns.column: Action required before recommendation is finalised

cell.column: Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised
Action required before recommendation is finalised

template_block.name: conditions-for-reconsideration
template_block.title: Conditions for Reconsideration
template_block.statement: Conditions that would shift the recommendation:

[LIST the specific evidence, findings, or events from Panel Section 4 that would cause the panel to recommend differently. Be specific enough that the SRO can monitor for these conditions during delivery.]

template_block.name: sro-decision-record
template_block.title: SRO Decision Record
template_block.statement: Completed by the SRO after this summary and the full transcript have been reviewed. This section is the decision record. It must not be left blank.

SRO name and role: [NAME, ROLE] Decision date: [YYYY-MM-DD] Decision: [ACCEPT RECOMMENDATION / ACCEPT WITH MODIFICATIONS — state modifications / REJECT — state reason and alternative] Rationale: [SRO's reasoning for the decision. If accepting the recommendation, state that the conditions are understood and owned. If modifying or rejecting, explain the basis.] Conditions ownership: [For each condition in the recommendation, name who is responsible for meeting it and by when.] ATRS record updated: [Yes — [date] / Not applicable — see ATRS note in full transcript]

This summary is not a decision. The SRO decision record above constitutes the decision. Full transcript reference: [FILENAME]. Retain both documents for the same period as the decision (minimum 6 years).
